name: Stripe API
what_it_does: The developer's payment platform with APIs for accepting payments, managing subscriptions, handling billing, and fraud prevention, with an official MCP server.
use_cases: Processing payments and managing customer billing from agents
not_for: Countries where Stripe is not available (many emerging markets)
Micropayments requiring very low transaction fees
best_when: An agent needs to manage payments, subscriptions, or billing for a SaaS or e-commerce product.
avoid_when: You operate in a country Stripe doesn't support, or have very high-risk business activities.

auth.notes: Secret key for server-side (agent) use, publishable key for client side. Restricted keys with fine-grained permissions recommended for agents. Test vs live mode keys clearly separated.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No monthly fee; pay per transaction only
pricing.paid_tiers: 2.9% + $0.30 per successful card charge, lower for volume
pricing.notes: No monthly fees - pure pay-per-transaction. Test mode is completely free. One of the most developer-friendly payment APIs.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Stripe has the best error messages in the payments industry. Structured errors with type, code, message, param, and doc_url. Every error links to specific documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on all write operations via Idempotency-Key header. Critical for agent retry safety.
known_agent_gotchas: Test vs live mode keys are easy to mix up - always validate key prefix (sk_test vs sk_live)
Idempotency keys are valid for 24 hours - agents that retry after 24h will create duplicate charges
Webhook signature verification is mandatory in production - agents receiving webhooks must verify
Restricted API key permissions must cover all operations the agent performs
Some operations (refund after 90 days, disputed charge after window) are time-limited
